subject: [PATCH v3 1/2] syscall_user_dispatch: Make it configurable in Kconfig

2026-07-06.patch_summary: This patch adds a new Kconfig option, CONFIG_SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH, to make the Syscall User Dispatch feature configurable. The feature allows a thread's system calls to be intercepted and redirected to a userspace signal handler based on a prctl() configured instruction pointer range. The patch also updates various kernel files to use the new configuration option instead of relying on the existing CONFIG_GENERIC_SYSCALL setting. This change makes it possible for users to disable Syscall User Dispatch independently, rather than having it built-in under the generic syscall configuration.
